大家日常使用开源程序时，一定要特别小心后门。

尤其是那种代码写得特别复杂，

然后加一个docker化入口让你直接一键部署的。

这种项目有90%的概率存在后门，尤其是交易相关、web3相关、存储客户敏感信息（如可能有收货地址）的代码。

CLI之所以如此令人兴奋，恰恰是因为它们是一种“传统”技术，这意味着AI代理可以原生且轻松地使用它们，并能将它们组合起来，通过整个终端工具包与它们进行交互。

例如，您可以让Claude/Codex代理安装这个新的Polymarket

RT @godofprompt: I finally understand why "act as an expert" prompts are destroying your results.

After 200+ tests across Claude, ChatGPT,…

Asking AI to "be creative" is the laziest prompt you can write.

And it produces the laziest output.

After 3 years of daily ChatGPT use, I cracked the structure that actually unlocks original, unexpected, usable creative work.

Here's the exact framework 👇 https://t.co/vvBn4GcTog

First, understand WHY "be creative" fails.

AI creativity is probabilistic. It defaults to the most statistically common answer.

"Be creative" has no constraints.
No constraints = no creative pressure.
No pressure = average output.

The fix isn't less structure. It's MORE of the

The 4-part Creative Unlock Structure:

→ FORM: Specify the exact format with one unusual constraint
→ LENS: Give it a specific perspective or voice it wouldn't default to
→ TENSION: Define two opposing forces it must resolve
→ ANTI-PATTERN: Tell it explicitly what it must NOT

FORM example:

❌ "Write me a tagline for my SaaS product"

✅ "Write a tagline under 6 words that sounds like it belongs in a 1970s noir film, not a tech product page"

The unusual constraint forces the model off its default path.

That's where original work lives. https://t.co/M8jV0zSdx4

LENS example:

❌ "Write a LinkedIn post about productivity"

✅ "Write a LinkedIn post about productivity from the perspective of someone who thinks hustle culture destroyed their marriage and rebuilt their life after slowing down"

Specific POV = specific voice = content nobody https://t.co/7CqPbCPRDv

TENSION example:

❌ "Write a product description"

✅ "Write a product description that makes the reader feel both urgency to buy AND slight guilt about wanting it"

Two opposing emotional forces = inherent drama.

Inherent drama = copy that moves people. https://t.co/Uyj9tSX0ha

ANTI-PATTERN example:

❌ "Write a creative intro for my newsletter"

✅ "Write a creative intro for my newsletter. Do NOT start with a question, a statistic, a personal story, or the word 'I'. No motivational energy. No life lessons."

Eliminating defaults forces invention. https://t.co/lim8IMOmZw

Full structure in action:

"Write a cold email for a B2B SaaS tool [FORM: in under 90 words, structured like a haiku expanded into prose] [LENS: from the perspective of someone who hates email] [TENSION: that feels urgent but completely unbothered] [ANTI-PATTERN: no features, no

Let me tell why this works...

Creativity = constraint + pressure + surprise.

Generic prompts remove all three.

This structure injects all three simultaneously.

Here.. you are not asking the LLM to be creative, you're building a box so specific that the only way out is through

Most people fight AI defaults with vague instructions.

"Be more creative."
"Make it less generic."
"Try again."

Stop negotiating. Start engineering.

Constraints aren't the enemy of creativity.

They're the only reason creativity exists.

Save this.

Your premium AI bundle to 10x your business

→ Prompts for marketing & business
→ Unlimited custom prompts
→ n8n automations
→ Weekly updates

Start your free trial👇
https://t.co/C8Zzw2Jkpc

As someone who has spent a lot of time with large companies talking about AI, I can say fairly confidently that no big organizational changes happened as a result of AI in 2025  I don’t think that tells us anything much about what will happen over the next couple years, though
It was the year, however, where ROI became positive. That, and agents, are why bigger changes are coming.
